content: Behavioral and Psychological Symptoms of Dementia (BPSD) encompass a heterogeneous range of psychiatric symptoms and behaviors occurring in patients with dementia. Symptoms include psychosis (delusions, hallucinations), mood disturbances (depression, anxiety, euphoria), agitation/aggression, apathy, disinhibition, irritability/lability, aberrant motor behavior (pacing, wandering), sleep disturbances, and appetite changes. Affects up to 90% of dementia patients over disease course. BPSD causes significant distress, caregiver burden, institutionalization risk, and reduced quality of life. Pathophysiology involves neurodegeneration in specific brain regions, neurotransmitter imbalances (acetylcholine, serotonin, dopamine), unmet needs, environmental triggers.
